content.problemStatement:
Nadaca is a country with NNN cities. These cities are numbered 111 through NNN and connected by MMM bidirectional roads. Each city can be reached from every other city using these roads.

Initially, Ryan is in city 111. At each of the following KKK seconds, he may move from his current city to an adjacent city (a city connected by a road to his current city) or stay at his current city. Ryan also has QQQ conditions (a1,b1),(a2,b2),‚Ä¶,(aQ,bQ)(a_1, b_1), (a_2, b_2), \ldots, (a_Q, b_Q)(a1‚Äã,b1‚Äã),(a2‚Äã,b2‚Äã),‚Ä¶,(aQ‚Äã,bQ‚Äã) meaning that during this KKK-second trip, for each valid iii, he wants to be in city aia_iai‚Äã after exactly bib_ibi‚Äã seconds.

Since you are very good with directions, Ryan asked you to tell him how many different trips he could make while satisfying all conditions. Compute this number modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7. A trip is a sequence of Ryan's current cities after 1,2,‚Ä¶,K1, 2, \ldots, K1,2,‚Ä¶,K seconds.
constraints: 1‚â§ui,vi‚â§N1 \le u_i, v_i \le N1‚â§ui‚Äã,vi‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
ui‚âÝviu_i \neq v_iui‚ÄãÓÄÝ=vi‚Äã for each valid iii
there is at most one road between each pair of cities
each city is reachable from every other city
1‚â§ai‚â§N1 \le a_i \le N1‚â§ai‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
0‚â§bi‚â§K0 \le b_i \le K0‚â§bi‚Äã‚â§K for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 9,0009,0009,000
the sum of KKK over all test cases does not exceed 9,0009,0009,000
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 9,0009,0009,000
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 9,0009,0009,000
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 400400400
the sum of KKK over all test cases does not exceed 400400400
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 400400400
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 400400400
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
Each of the next MMM lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting a road between cities uuu and vvv.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ.
QQQ lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§Q1 \le i \le Q1‚â§i‚â§Q), the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers aia_iai‚Äã and bib_ibi‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of trips Ryan can make, modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
